resources.string: Direct play ASS subtitles
Experimental feature, may not work reliably
LibASS options
Renderer and cache switches for ASS/SSA direct play. Changes apply to newly started playback.
Advertise embedded and external ASS/SSA subtitle support for direct play. When disabled, the server may transcode or burn these subtitles.
Renderer
Choose how LibASS output is sent to Media3.
OpenGL overlay
Fast overlay renderer and the default choice. Use a fallback if subtitles disappear or the device GPU has issues.
Canvas overlay
CPU-backed overlay renderer. It can help devices where OpenGL subtitles fail, but may cost more CPU.
Media3 cues
Feeds rendered subtitle cues through Media3. Use as a compatibility fallback; advanced ASS styling may be less accurate.
Maximum render pixels
Limit the subtitle render surface to reduce memory and CPU cost on heavy ASS tracks.
Full surface
No render limit. Best quality, highest memory use on 4K screens.
720p cap
Cap rendering around 1280 x 720. Fastest fallback, but subtitles can look softer.
1080p cap
Cap rendering around 1920 x 1080. Good fallback for 4K TVs that struggle with complex ASS effects.
1440p cap
Cap rendering around 2560 x 1440. Balances sharpness and memory use on high-resolution screens.
4K cap
Cap rendering around 3840 x 2160. Keeps full 4K quality with a hard ceiling.
Bitmap cache
Controls LibASS bitmap cache memory. Larger values can reduce rerendering on complex signs and karaoke.
32 MB
Lowest memory use. Try this if playback is memory constrained.
64 MB
Moderate memory use for simpler ASS tracks.
128 MB
Balanced cache size for most ASS/SSA subtitles.
256 MB
Default cache size for heavy ASS tracks. Can smooth complex effects but uses more memory.
Glyph cache
Controls how many shaped glyphs LibASS keeps cached. Larger values help subtitle-heavy anime and styled signs.
2,500 glyphs
Lowest glyph cache. Use when memory is tight.
5,000 glyphs
Moderate glyph cache for lighter ASS/SSA subtitle tracks.
10,000 glyphs
Balanced glyph cache for most styled subtitles.
20,000 glyphs
Default glyph cache for heavy ASS tracks. Helps very complex subtitle tracks but uses more memory.
Experimental subtitle extraction
Parse subtitles in the Media3 extractor path.

Disabled by default because subtitle offset is applied in the renderer; when this is enabled, subtitle offset controls will not move embedded extracted subtitles. External subtitles still use the renderer path.
